channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ogun 2027: Yayi Youth Vanguard targets 400,000 votes for Adeola’s governorship bid
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Women leader, APC, Ijebu Ode Local government, Mrs. Ajayi Olusola, appealed to Senator Adeola to prioritise women and youth participation in governance if elected, while commending Governor Dapo Abiodun for promoting gender inclusion in his administration.

item.title: Tinubu Felicitates Insurance Mogul, Senator Yinka Omilani At 90
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: –         Rejoices with ex-Lagos lawmaker, Segun Olulade on 55th birthday Deji Elumoye in Abuja President Bola Tinubu has felicitated former Senator and successful insurance mogul, Yinka Omilani, as he marks his

item.description: Mary Nnah The 9th Lagos Leather Fair closed recently with a clear roadmap for the continent’s leather industry: comply with global standards, adopt artificial intelligence, and build for export. Over

item.title: SBI Funds Poised for Strong India Debut After $1 Billion IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is poised for a strong trading debut Tuesday after investors piled into its $1 billion initial public offering, making it one of India’s most heavily subscribed billion-dollar share sales.

item.title: Was the World Cup 2026 A Success?

item.title: Kogi L-PRES launches campaign on mass artificial insemination
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The State Project Coordinator (SPC), Kogi L-PRES, Mr Otaru Abdulkabir Onoruoyiza, said the Mass AI Program was designed to provide livestock farmers with access to superior genetics and technical support to improve meat and milk production, growth rates, reproduction, and disease resilience.

item.title: Two years after: How NALDA is redefining Nigeria’s agricultural future
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Cornelius Adebayo, had spent exactly two years as Executive Secretary of the National Agricultural Land Development Authority (NALDA) when he reflected on the agency’s journey under his leadership. Rather than celebrating the milestone with a list of completed projects, he offered a broader vision o

item.title: Yoruba group calls for unity, farmers’ security, cultural heritage
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Association of Ajoro Omo Oduduwa has appealed to Yoruba home and in the diaspora to embrace unity, peace and justice, saying these values are essential for preserving the cultural heritage of the race. In a statement by its founder, Babatunde Balogun (Jagun Aso Pedepede), and general-secretary,

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Authorities detain ferry crew members after drug tests reveal violations in Guyana's worst marine disaster in decades.

item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has dismissed media reports insinuating that the Comptroller General of the Nigeria Immigration Service, Kemi Nandap, is being investigated over alleged visa racketeering, saying it has no case whatsoever against her. item.description: Despite their absence from the 2026 World Cup, Nigeria’s  Super Eagles have maintained their position on the latest FIFA Rankings released on Monday. The Super Eagles remain the 26th best

item.title: FIFA rankings: Nigeria drop to 4th in Africa
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s Super Eagles slip to 4th in Africa in the latest FIFA rankings despite retaining 26th globally, following Egypt’s strong World Cup performance.

item.description:

item.title: Erimope-Ekiti best 2026 UTME candidate bags N1m scholarship
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Miss Daniella Owoeye of Erinmope-Ekiti, Ekiti State, has received a ₦1million scholarship in recognition of her outstanding performance in the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME), where she scored 372 to emerge as the highest scorer nationwide. The scholarship was awarded by Ekiti

item.title: Enugu to distribute agric inputs for 63,000 farmers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Enugu State Government will, today, distribute farm inputs to 63,000 smallholder farmers across the state to boost agricultural production this farming season. The post Enugu to distribute agric inputs for 63,000 farmers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: Wake him up at midnight or just before cock-crow, and demand of him a blueprint on solutions to the challenges confronting the Niger Delta, he will do so with smashing coherence and deep insight. item.description: South Korean investors slashed their leveraged stock positions to the lowest level in three months, as memory-chip losses halted the market’s rally.

item.title: How thunderstorm claimed 6 children
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A thunderstorm has killed six children from the same family and injured four others in Dadiri-Tirgali village, Garum District of Ganye Local Government Area of Adamawa State. The incident occurred at about 4:00 p.m. on Sunday during a heavy downpour, Daily Trust gathered. The victims, according to f

item.title: Okin seeks unity, service as he takes over Rotary District 9126 leadership
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE new District Governor of Rotary International District 9126, Olaniyi Okin, has pledged to strengthen the district through membership growth, support for the Rotary Foundation, polio eradication efforts, and community service. Speaking at his investiture in Ibadan, Oyo State, on Saturday, Okin de

item.title: FG, BoA, the firm target 25 million tonnes grain output
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government and the Bank of Agriculture (BOA) and an agricultural firm, Arzikin Noma, are set to launch a nationwide agricultural financing programme aimed at boosting grain production and empowering smallholder farmers. The initiative targets increasing annual grain output to 25 million

item.title: Global Stocks Lift Australian Pension Funds to 9.5% Annual Gain
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A rally in global equities delivered another strong year for Australia’s A$4.4 trillion ($3.1 trillion) pension fund industry, despite lingering concerns over the Middle East conflict and stubborn inflation.

item.title: El-Rufai’s doctor faces panel over alleged procurement of medical report
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Dr Bello Abubakar, the personal physician to a former governor of Kaduna State, Nasir El-Rufai, has been quizzed by the Medical and Dental Consultants’ Association of Nigeria (MDCAN) over alleged involvement in the procurement of fake medical report on behalf of his patient, Daily Trust has learnt.

item.title: Sanwo-Olu Presents N3.1bn Compensation to 331 Lagos Property Owners
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, has presented N3.1 billion in compensation to 331 property owners whose lands and buildings were acquired for major infrastructure projects across the state. Sanwo-Olu disclosed this in a statement shared on his official X handle on Monday, July 20, saying t

item.title: U.S. hits Canada with stiff new tariffs, escalating trade tensions
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday said it would slap 50% tariffs on some goods imported from Canada, in an effort to end what it sees as discriminatory trade practices by Ottawa against the U.S. automobile, dairy and alcohol industries.

item.title: 3 years after Niger coup: Rethinking Nigeria’s relations with Niamey
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Three years ago, on July 26, 2023, soldiers led by General Abdourahamane Tchiani overthrew the democratically elected government of President Mohammed Bazoum, plunging Niger into its deepest policy crisis in decades. The coup not only ended one of the Sahel’s few remaining constitutional governments

item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: The everyday lessons Kannywood owes society
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By Ibrahim A. Gurin THE Kannywood film industry is more than an entertainment industry. It has become one of the most powerful cultural influences in Northern Nigeria. The stories it tells shape how people think, speak, and sometimes even behave. Characters become role models, and the habits they di

item.title: More than 100 missing as deadly flooding hits Afghanistan
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: More than 100 people are missing after deadly flooding hit Afghanistan’s Nuristan province on Monday, disaster officials and the governor’s office said. Twenty bodies have been recovered and more than 80 people injured, Afghanistan’s disaster agency ANDMA said. The casualties were reported in the pr

item.title: Gold Steady as Traders Watch Middle East War for Inflation Clues
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold was steady as traders monitored a raft of developments in the Middle East conflict for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

item.title: Guyana ferry capsizes, leaving at least 27 dead and 83 missing
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 27 people died after a ferry capsized off Guyana, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday, as rescuers searched for 83 people still missing. Sixty-nine passengers have been rescued since the MV Barima overturned Saturday night while traveling from Georgetown to the inland Essequibo region,

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria sits on one of the largest gas reserves in Africa, roughly 216 trillion cubic feet by government estimates, and read more Reserves alone won’t power Nigeria’s energy ambitions

item.title: FULL LIST: 12 Major Finals Cristiano Ronaldo Has Lost
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has built one of the most successful careers in football history, winning league titles in England, Spain and Italy, lifting five UEFA Champions League trophies and captaining Portugal to their first major international titles. His remarkable consistency has seen him feature in doz

item.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

item.title: What are the new British Prime Minister’s biggest challenges?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Britain has a new Prime Minister: Andy Burnham. The former Mayor of Manchester must now unite a divided nation.

item.title: STAC Marine strengthens brand after $20m Abo FPSO acquisition
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Co-Founder and Creative Director of Flip Visuals, Austine Nwaojei, said STAC Marine understood from the outset the importance of establishing a strong corporate identity as it expanded its operations.

item.title: Nigeria Inaugurates SAF C’ttee, Targets Role As Africa’s Sustainable Aviation Fuel Hub
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As FG unveils feasibility study, launches ICAO-backed business plan for aviation decarbonisation The Nigerian government on Monday took a major step toward decarbonising its aviation sector with the inauguration of a National High-Level Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Steering Committee and t

item.title: Menzies shocks world No 2 Humphries in World Matchplay thriller
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies produced a massive upset to defeat 2024 champion Luke Humphries 10-7 at the World Matchplay on Monday night.

item.title: ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US governmentAs part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers”

item.title: New Zealand’s Faster Inflation Bolsters Case for More Rate Hikes
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, fanned by soaring fuel prices and bolstering the case for the Reserve Bank to execute back-to-back interest-rate increases.

item.title: HUGE SHOCK! Humphries OUT after Big Fish finish from Menzies!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the first-round clash between Luke Humphries and Cameron Menzies at the World Matchplay.
